title: Nassella gigantea
taxonomy: Nassella gigantea is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is classified within the tribe Stipeae. This classification places Nassella gigantea among other related grass species. Nassella gigantea is further classified as Nassella gigantea (Kunth) Romewell & D.I. Morris, indicating its specific binomial nomenclature. The classification of Nassella gigantea is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.

side_effects: Nassella gigantea, commonly known as giant needlegrass, is not widely documented in relation to potential side effects, likely due to its limited use as an ornamental grass in landscaping. intro: Nassella gigantea, commonly known as Giant Needlegrass, is a type of perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Nassella gigantea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
